county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War damage — some early records lost or damaged
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Prince William Circuit Court (Manassas). Northern Virginia county formed 1731. Two major Civil War battles of Manassas (Bull Run) — some records damaged. Manassas National Battlefield Park records at NPS. Marine Corps Base Quantico covers part of county. RELIC (Ruth E. Lloyd Information Center) at Bull Run Regional Library is an excellent genealogical resource.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Pulaski Circuit Court (Pulaski). New River valley county formed 1839 from Wythe and Montgomery. Radford Arsenal/Army Ammunition Plant records. Good surviving records. Check Wythe and Montgomery for earlier records. Pulaski County Historical Society. New River and Claytor Lake area. Railroad heritage — Norfolk & Western records at Virginia Tech.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Virginia independent city since 1892; carved from Montgomery County
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Radford city records. Became independent from Montgomery County 1892. Radford University archives. Radford Army Ammunition Plant records. New River area. Check Montgomery County for pre-1892 records. Glencoe Mansion Museum. Good modern city records.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Rappahannock Circuit Court (Washington). Piedmont county formed 1833 from Culpeper — named for the old extinct Rappahannock County. Good surviving records. Small rural county. Shenandoah National Park displaced some western communities. Rappahannock Historical Society. Check Culpeper for pre-1833 records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Virginia independent city since 1871; carved from Henrico County
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Richmond Circuit Court. Capital of Virginia since 1780 and Confederate capital 1861-65. LVA is physically in Richmond — all state records accessible. Richmond city Hustings Court records at LVA are critical. Valentine Museum, Virginia Museum of History and Culture, Black History Museum and Cultural Center. Richmond city directories from 1819.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Richmond County Circuit Court (Warsaw). Northern Neck county formed 1692 from old Rappahannock (extinct). Good colonial record survival. Northern Neck Proprietary land records at LVA. Warsaw was a colonial tobacco port. Richmond County Museum. FamilySearch has digitized LVA microfilm. Not to be confused with Richmond city.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Roanoke County Circuit Court (Salem). Valley county formed 1838 from Botetourt. Roanoke city carved out 1884, Salem 1968. Good surviving records. Virginia Room at Roanoke Public Library has regional genealogical collections. Historical Society of Western Virginia. Norfolk & Western Railway records at Virginia Tech.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Virginia independent city since 1884; carved from Roanoke County
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Roanoke city Circuit Court. Became independent from Roanoke County 1884. Virginia Room at Roanoke Public Library is a major regional genealogical resource. Historical Society of Western Virginia. Norfolk & Western Railway records at Virginia Tech (headquarters city). Check Roanoke County for pre-1884 and surrounding area families.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Rockbridge Circuit Court (Lexington). Shenandoah Valley county formed 1778 from Augusta and Botetourt. Washington and Lee University (Leyburn Library Special Collections) and VMI archives have exceptional local records. Natural Bridge area. Rockbridge Historical Society. Scots-Irish settlement — Timber Ridge and New Providence Presbyterian Church records.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Rockingham Circuit Court (Harrisonburg). Major Shenandoah Valley county formed 1778 from Augusta. Harrisonburg carved out 1916. German Mennonite and Brethren communities — Eastern Mennonite University archives have extensive genealogical records. Rockingham County Historical Society. Massanutten settlement records. JMU Special Collections.
